attacking_style: Launches long-range solo attacks (50km+) on flat to rolling stages, maintaining gaps of 1:30+ through sustained power output rather than explosive accelerations. Thrives in breakaway scenarios where sprinter teams misjudge timing.
team_tactics: Operates with significant independence in breakaways, relying on peloton miscalculation rather than team setups. No clear domestique support pattern visible in successful efforts.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling stages with crosswind exposure where sustainable power matters more than climbing. Excels when sprinter teams must control but hesitate, creating tactical confusion.
vulnerability: Gets caught in final kilometer when sprinter teams finally commit to organized chase. Requires peloton disorganization or miscalculation to succeed rather than pure strength advantage.
key_patterns: Executes 40-50km solo efforts maintaining 1:30 gaps through time trial-style riding (2 observations)
Succeeds when sprinter teams delay chase until final 10km or less (2 observations)
Wins from small tactical breakaways when stronger riders underestimate his finishing speed (2 observations)
Exploits crosswind conditions to establish and maintain breakaway gaps (2 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: Won stage and showed improved sprint finish while demonstrating exceptional endurance in 50km solo effort within same Grand Tour
